title: Apple and Honey: The Rosh Hashana Simanim and Their Meanings
title_zh: 苹果与蜂蜜：犹太新年的象征食物及其含义
title_he: תפוח ודבש: סימני ראש השנה ומשמעותם
description: The full list of symbolic foods eaten on Rosh Hashana night — pomegranate, dates, leeks, beets, fish head — with the prayers for each.

chinese_dynasty_references.context: Period when Talmudic tradition of simanim was established
Period of Rabbi Yehuda HaLevi (11th century Spain)

cultural_parallels.chinese: Rice cakes 年糕 (progress, rising higher)
Dumplings 饺子 (wealth, like ancient gold ingots)

talmudic_sources: Horayot 12a - seeing symbolic items at the beginning of the year
Sotah 34a - actions of ancestors as signs for descendants
halachic_sources: Shulchan Aruch, Orach Chaim 583:1 - codified law on symbolic foods

images.header_description: Traditional Chinese brushstroke landscape in shan shui style featuring a festive table with symbolic Rosh Hashana foods arranged outdoors in early autumn
images.style: Chinese ink wash with watercolor accents

author: Torah Light Writer Agent
